subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 21
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known to be the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah can reach speeds of up to 70 miles per hour in short bursts, making it the fastest land animal.
questions.hint: It has black spots and is a big cat.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Egypt?
questions.explanation: Cairo is the bustling capital city of Egypt, located near the famous pyramids.

questions.q: Which part of the plant grows underground and absorbs water?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant in the soil and absorb water and nutrients necessary for growth.
questions.hint: They are hidden beneath the earth.

questions.q: What do we call an animal that eats only plants?
questions.explanation: An herbivore is an animal adapted to eat plant material as its main food source.

questions.q: Which of these is important for a balanced and healthy diet?
options: Eating a variety of fruits and vegetables
questions.answer: Eating a variety of fruits and vegetables
questions.explanation: Fruits and vegetables provide essential vitamins and minerals that our bodies need to stay healthy.
questions.hint: These are colorful foods from nature.

questions.q: Which community helper works in a hospital and cares for patients?
questions.explanation: Nurses are healthcare professionals who work to provide patient care and support doctors.
questions.hint: They check your temperature and heart rate.

questions.q: What is the name of the planet closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and is the closest to the Sun.
questions.hint: It has a name like a Roman god.

questions.q: Which of these materials is a conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Metals like copper and aluminum are good conductors because they allow electricity to flow through them easily.
questions.hint: Pots and pans are often made of this.

questions.q: What is the primary gas in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the Earth's atmosphere.
Spring follows winter and is known for blooming flowers and warming weather.
